history remembers Alexander as \"the Great,\" a critical examination reveals that his achievements were built almost entirely upon the foundations laid by his father, Philip II of Macedon. From the revolutionary phalanx formation to a professional standing army and a treasury flush with gold, Alexander inherited the tools of conquest rather than forging them himself."}],"$L3"]}]]}]]}],"$L4"]}],["$L5"],"$L6"]}],"isPartial":false,"staleTime":300,"varyParams":null,"buildId":"tm2aUIQ57RD4gYx6Y-Ve9"}
